title: Haenelia scabrida
intro: Haenelia scabrida, also known as Slender Haenelia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Haeneliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Haenelia scabrida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Haenelia scabrida include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Haenelia scabrida is classified as a species of lichen, specifically belonging to the family Roccellaceae. It is a type of crustose lichen, characterized by its growth on rocks and other substrates. The genus Haenelia is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes various species of lichens. Further classification includes the kingdom Fungi and the division Ascomycota. Haenelia scabrida is a unique species within the Roccellaceae family.
